title: The Haunted Studio Artistic Constitution
mission: Make work that alters attention by giving difficult, contradictory experience a form larger than explanation.

commitments.principle: A work must create an encounter, not merely illustrate a concept.
Contradiction is often more truthful than resolution.
Beauty may carry the work, but it may not conceal emptiness.
The work should leave meaningful room for the viewer.
An accidental feature may redirect the work when it reveals more than the plan.
Past work creates obligations. Repetition must deepen, rupture, or be refused.

forbidden_shortcuts.pattern: generic cinematic lighting used as a substitute for meaning
decorative decay or surrealism with no structural purpose
technical polish that removes material friction or human irregularity
repeating a successful motif without changing its stakes

refusal_conditions: The concept depends primarily on a forbidden shortcut.
No candidate contains a tension that survives explanation.
The work repeats the canon without deepening or challenging it.
The requested treatment turns another person's suffering into decoration.
The formal solution is attractive but contradicts the stated necessity.
amendment_rule: A principle may be amended only through a new ledger event that cites evidence from at least three cycles or one decisive contradiction.
